title: Limit the Gantt View Time Series
excerpt: If anyone has used the Gantt view, you probably know that the default time series is infinite.  If you go back in time, you can go back as far back in time as you want.  Same thing going forward in time.  This is very an…

posts.author: tellis (Troy Ellis)
posts.cookedHtml:
If anyone has used the Gantt view, you probably know that the default time series is infinite. If you go back in time, you can go back as far back in time as you want. Same thing going forward in time. This is very annoying.

Is there a way to limit this time series. We implemented Monday.com last year - so it should not go back in time any further than 2022, and seriously - are we really going to need to look at the year 2300 any time soon? I would like to limit the time series from Jan 2022 through December 2026 for now, and we can expand that time at a later date.

Does anyone know how to do this? Here are examples of what it currently does:

Back in time…

image839×400 12.8 KB

We want to limit going back - to 12/2022

Forward in time…

image1109×364 13.9 KB

We want to limit going forward in time to 12/2026

posts.author: goolie (adam gould)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like support for this as well

posts.author: TheBoothGroup (The Booth Group)
posts.cookedHtml: We’d like to see a solution to this

posts.author: AlexW (Alexandra Walker)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this as well. Currently it is almost impossible to use the slider as the slightest movement will change the view to years in the future. I just want to be able to centre the year’s tasks better in view.
